subject: Re: Album cover commission — Dawnstone / The Wren Brothers
body:
Benny — listened through the demo tracks twice. Really sparse and evocative. Here's what I'm thinking for the rough concept:

- Roadside diner at dusk, neon sign half-busted, kudzu creeping up the wall
- Two silhouetted figures in the parking lot, backs to viewer
- Color palette: dusty ochre sky, cool blue-green neon spill, deep shadow browns

I'll have pencil sketches by December 13 as promised. Should I also include a type treatment option or will your art director handle lettering?

Henry

subject: Re: Feedback — pp 20–28 follow-up + pp 29–35 ETA
body:
Prof. Hargrove,

Quick update: I've redrawn p22 panel 3 with a higher angle and it reads much cleaner. I also tightened the lettering on p26 — reduced one balloon by about 30% and the crowd detail breathes now.

Pages 29–35 are penciled, about 60% inked. I'm aiming to send you the full batch by January 28 if that works. Let me know if the timeline is okay.

subject: Re: Chapter 3 structure question
body: Dennis — the recurring visual anchor. That's the note I needed. What if it's the same mug, moving between hands, appearing in both timelines — a small object doing quiet load-bearing work.

subject: Foxhollow submission — cover letter draft
body: To the editors at Foxhollow — please find enclosed the first 30 pages of The Liminal Market, a literary graphic novel in progress. The book takes place across three nights at a market that appears in a Nashville lot between a gentrifying block and one holding on...

subject: Re: Dawnstone fall LP — demo notes
body: Benny — yeah I heard the water imagery too. Thinking a broken window with rain streaks pooling on the sill inside. Fall-heavy palette, wet blacks. Let me send a rough by end of next week.
